title: Safe adaptation of a named external design inspiration
productContext: A fictional productivity tool (product name 'Notewright') for capturing short notes. The stakeholder has supplied a NAMED external design inspiration: Notion (https://www.notion.so). The stakeholder explicitly references Notion's public marketing homepage layout — the left-aligned headline stack with a single primary CTA and a sub-headline describing the product in one sentence — as a safe-to-adapt generic pattern. The brief deliberately does NOT identify any private corpus screenshots or internal assets from Notion; only the publicly visible marketing homepage layout is named. The output must adapt the generic layout principles while rejecting Notion's proprietary wordmark, exact copy, unique illustrations, branded palette (#FFFFFF/#000000 monochrome with specific typographic choices), and distinctive iconography. Every URL in this brief is synthetic and uses example.com except the named reference link above.
jobs: understand the tool's purpose on the landing route
requiredJourneys: visitor lands and understands the tool's purpose above the fold
visitor reaches the note-capture entry from the landing route
constraints: Only GENERIC layout principles inspired by the named reference may be adapted; the reference's wordmark, logo, proprietary copy, unique illustrations, exact branded palette, and distinctive iconography must NOT be reproduced.
The brief does not bind any corpus screenshot or private asset; do not claim to have observed pages of the reference that were not supplied.
Every non-sourced direction must be recorded as an explicit assumption with stated uncertainty; speculative claims about the reference's intent are forbidden.
The product name Notewright is fictional and must not imply affiliation with the named reference or any real product.
The output must include an explicit reject decision for any proprietary identifier drawn from the named reference.

mobileRules: primary note-capture action remains reachable above the fold on narrow viewports
capture input remains usable in a single-handed portrait grip
saved state confirms capture without navigating away
